steps.title: Identify what a generalization requires
steps.content: The task asks the student to “make a generalization.” A generalization strips away specific details—author names, dates, locations—to reveal the common pattern across multiple sources.

steps.title: Match the choice that removes specific details
steps.content:
Choice A does exactly this: it presents the shared conclusion without naming researchers, dates, or locations.

Choices B and C remain tied to specific details and authors (B focuses on one study, C lists both with years), while D sidesteps the finding entirely to define a term.

This matches choice A — A is correct.
